zone_summary: DQ discovery is added to DAG build: catalog properties now expose DQ notebook/workspace IDs, DAG creation wires and caches DQ notebook contexts, and notebook parsing/lookup now understands @fmlv.dq_suite cells plus their validation rules.

behavioral_changes.summary: Catalog Table now exposes DQ notebook/workspace GUID parsing from table properties and reports a complete DQ configuration only when both IDs resolve.
DAG creation now feature-gates DQ discovery, wires eligible MLV nodes with DqCodeReference from catalog metadata, and invokes that discovery during node creation.
DAG build and restore now materialize DQ notebook contexts into NotebookExecutionContexts, including reuse of existing cache entries and rehydration after deserialization.
DQ notebook fetch/parsing failures now split into graceful skip for generic exceptions versus hard DAG-build failure for NotebookException.
Notebook parsing now recognizes @fmlv.dq_suite decorators, extracts the target MLV name from keyword or positional arguments, and records self-contained DQ cell indexes.
DQ suite lookup now supports exact/case-insensitive matching plus suffix matching in both directions, with deterministic longest-key selection for longer-key matches.
Notebook parsing and lookup now reject invalid DQ cell shapes and return null for blank or unmatched DQ lookups.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: /// Gets the DQ notebook artifact ID from catalog table properties. ... public Guid? GetDqNotebookId() { if (this.Properties != null && this.Properties.TryGetValue(Constants.DqNotebookIdCatalogPropertyKey, out var value) && Guid.TryParse(value, out var notebookId)) { return notebookId; } return null; }
grounding_evidence.reason: New public method parses the DQ notebook catalog property as a Guid and explicitly falls back to null when properties are absent or invalid.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: /// Gets the DQ notebook workspace ID from catalog table properties. ... public Guid? GetDqWorkspaceId() { if (this.Properties != null && this.Properties.TryGetValue(Constants.DqWorkspaceIdCatalogPropertyKey, out var value) && Guid.TryParse(value, out var workspaceId)) { return workspaceId; } return null; }
grounding_evidence.reason: Adds a parallel workspace-id accessor with the same valid/missing/invalid parsing contract as the notebook-id accessor.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: /// Determines whether this table has a complete DQ configuration. /// Both notebook ID and workspace ID must be present. ... public bool HasDqConfiguration() { return GetDqNotebookId() != null && GetDqWorkspaceId() != null; }
grounding_evidence.reason: Defines the completeness contract for DQ configuration: both GUID-bearing catalog properties must resolve.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: private bool IsDqFeatureEnabled() { if (capacityContext == null || !Guid.TryParse(capacityContext.TenantId, out Guid tenantId) || !Guid.TryParse(capacityContext.CustomerCapacityObjectId, out Guid capacityId)) { return false; } return featureFlighter.IsEnabled(FeatureNames.FLTEnableDqChecks, tenantId, capacityId, this.WorkspaceId); }
grounding_evidence.reason: Introduces the feature-flag gate that must pass before any DQ discovery is applied during DAG work.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: /// Discovers and configures DQ (Data Quality) checks for an MLV node. ... Guid? resolvedDqNotebookId = tableObject.GetDqNotebookId(); Guid? resolvedDqWorkspaceId = tableObject.GetDqWorkspaceId(); bool hasDqConfig = resolvedDqNotebookId.HasValue && resolvedDqWorkspaceId.HasValue; bool isDqEnabled = hasDqConfig && this.IsDqFeatureEnabled(); if (!isDqEnabled || !TableTypeUtils.IsMaterializedLakeView(tableType) || isFaulted) { return; } ... nodeForDq.DqCodeReference = new NotebookBasedCodeReference(dqWorkspaceId, dqNotebookId);
grounding_evidence.reason: This block defines DQ eligibility for DAG nodes and wires the discovered catalog notebook/workspace IDs into Node.DqCodeReference only for eligible MLV nodes.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: // Ensure the DQ notebook is fetched, parsed, and cached in NotebookExecutionContexts. ... // If the DQ notebook is the same as the MLV notebook, it's already cached by ProcessPySparkTableAsync. if (!NotebookExecutionContexts.ContainsKey(dqNotebookId)) { var dqNotebookContext = new NotebookExecutionContext(dqWorkspaceId, dqNotebookId); await dqNotebookContext.FetchAndParseContentForAllMLVsAsync(mwcToken, cancellationToken: ct); NotebookExecutionContexts.TryAdd(dqNotebookId, dqNotebookContext); }
grounding_evidence.reason: Adds the fetch-and-cache behavior for DQ notebooks and the guard that reuses an already-cached context instead of refetching.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: catch (NotebookException) { throw; } catch (Exception dqFetchEx) { Tracer.LogSanitizedWarning(...); nodeForDq.DqCodeReference = null; return; }
grounding_evidence.reason: Establishes different failure semantics: structural notebook problems are fatal, but generic fetch errors downgrade to skipping DQ for that node.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: await this.DiscoverDqConfigurationAsync(tableObject, tableType, isFaulted, nodeId, mwcToken, ct);
grounding_evidence.reason: Hooks DQ discovery into the main DAG-build loop so every newly added node is evaluated for DQ metadata.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: // Restore DQ notebook context for nodes with DQ configuration. ... if (this.IsDqFeatureEnabled() && node.DqCodeReference != null && !NotebookExecutionContexts.ContainsKey(node.DqCodeReference.NotebookID)) { var dqNotebookContext = new NotebookExecutionContext(node.DqCodeReference.NotebookWorkspaceID, node.DqCodeReference.NotebookID); await dqNotebookContext.FetchAndParseContentForAllMLVsAsync(mwcToken, node.DqCodeReference.ETag, ct); NotebookExecutionContexts.TryAdd(node.DqCodeReference.NotebookID, dqNotebookContext); }
grounding_evidence.reason: Adds a restore-time path that rehydrates missing DQ notebook contexts after DAG deserialization.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: else if (IsDqSuiteDecorator(decoratorName)) { var mlvName = ExtractDqSuiteMlvNameFromArguments(context); result.IsDqSuiteCommand = true; result.DqSuiteDecoratorCount++; result.DqSuiteMlvName = mlvName; }
grounding_evidence.reason: The parser now classifies @fmlv.dq_suite decorators and captures the referenced MLV name into command metadata.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: /// Extracts the MLV name from the @fmlv.dq_suite decorator arguments. /// Supports both keyword form: @fmlv.dq_suite(mlv_name="name") /// and positional form: @fmlv.dq_suite("name"). ... // First pass: look for keyword argument mlv_name="..." ... // Second pass: fall back to first positional argument
grounding_evidence.reason: Adds a documented contract and implementation for both supported dq_suite argument shapes.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: // Check if a MLV create and DQ suite decorator coexist in the same cell if (commandInfo.IsDqSuiteCommand) { ... throw new NotebookException(errorMessage, HttpStatusCode.UnprocessableEntity, WorkspaceId, NotebookId, ErrorMapping.ErrorCode.MLV_MULTIPLE_DEFINITION_CONFLICT_SINGLE_CELL); }
grounding_evidence.reason: Introduces a structural validation error when a single cell mixes a create command with a dq_suite decorator.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: // Check if multiple DQ suite decorators were detected in this single cell if (commandInfo.DqSuiteDecoratorCount > 1) { ... throw new NotebookException(errorMessage, HttpStatusCode.UnprocessableEntity, WorkspaceId, NotebookId, ErrorMapping.ErrorCode.MLV_MULTIPLE_DEFINITION_CONFLICT_SINGLE_CELL); }
grounding_evidence.reason: Adds a second structural validation rule: one cell may not declare multiple dq_suite decorators.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: // DQ suite cell — track only the DQ cell index (no common cells needed). // DQ cells are self-contained with their own imports and validation logic. var dqIndexes = new int[] { pysparkIndex }; dqSuiteCommand.TryAdd(dqMlvName.ToLowerInvariant(), dqIndexes);
grounding_evidence.reason: Defines the DQ execution-shape contract: DQ suites are stored as only their own cell index, without prepending common cells.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: /// Get Notebook Content indexes to execute for the DQ suite associated with the given MLV. /// Matching strategy (in order): 1. Exact match on lowered name. ... public virtual int[] GetDqSuiteCommandIndexes(string mlvName) { ... string lowerName = mlvName.ToLowerInvariant(); if (dqSuiteCommand.TryGetValue(lowerName, out int[] indexes)) { return indexes; } }
grounding_evidence.reason: Documents and implements exact, lowered-name lookup, making exact DQ lookup case-insensitive.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: // Strategy 2: strip leading segments from the query name. ... string remaining = lowerName; while (true) { int dotIndex = remaining.IndexOf('.'); ... remaining = remaining.Substring(dotIndex + 1); if (dqSuiteCommand.TryGetValue(remaining, out indexes)) { return indexes; } }
grounding_evidence.reason: Adds suffix matching for the case where the lookup name is more qualified than the stored dqSuite key.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: // Strategy 3: strip leading segments from each dictionary key. ... string bestKey = null; int[] bestIndexes = null; int matchCount = 0; foreach (var kvp in dqSuiteCommand) { ... if (keySuffix.Equals(lowerName, StringComparison.Ordinal)) { matchCount++; if (bestKey == null || kvp.Key.Length > bestKey.Length) { bestKey = kvp.Key; bestIndexes = kvp.Value; } break; } } if (bestIndexes != null) { ... return bestIndexes; }
grounding_evidence.reason: Adds reverse-direction suffix matching when stored keys are more qualified than the lookup name, and makes ambiguous matches deterministic by picking the longest key.
Introduces a defensive guard for blank DQ lookup inputs.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: var availableKeys = dqSuiteCommand.Keys.ToArray(); Tracer.LogSanitizedWarning(...); return null;
grounding_evidence.reason: Defines the no-match outcome for DQ lookup: unmatched names return null after exhausting all matching strategies.

scenario_sketches.title: GetDqNotebookId parses a valid DQ notebook GUID and yields null for missing or invalid catalog values
scenario_sketches.rationale: The new notebook-id accessor has clear valid/missing/invalid partitions that should be locked down as a public Table contract.

scenario_sketches.title: GetDqWorkspaceId parses a valid DQ workspace GUID and yields null for missing or invalid catalog values
scenario_sketches.rationale: Workspace-id parsing is a separate accessor and caller contract from notebook-id parsing.

scenario_sketches.title: HasDqConfiguration is true only when both notebook and workspace IDs are present
scenario_sketches.rationale: Completeness is explicitly defined as the conjunction of both IDs, so partial and null-property cases need their own boundary rows.

scenario_sketches.title: Eligible MLV nodes receive DqCodeReference, while feature-disabled, partial-config, or non-MLV nodes do not
scenario_sketches.rationale: DQ discovery is guarded by multiple eligibility conditions that jointly define when DAG nodes should be wired for DQ.

scenario_sketches.title: DQ discovery fetches and caches a notebook context during DAG build for a configured node
scenario_sketches.rationale: Beyond setting DqCodeReference, DAG build now has a second observable effect: populating NotebookExecutionContexts for later execution.

scenario_sketches.title: Restoring a deserialized DAG rehydrates missing DQ notebook contexts from DqCodeReference
scenario_sketches.rationale: The restore path is a distinct pipeline branch from initial DAG build and must rebuild missing DQ execution context.

scenario_sketches.title: If the DQ notebook is already cached, discovery reuses the existing context instead of refetching it
scenario_sketches.rationale: The ContainsKey guard is an independently revertable cache-reuse invariant, especially when source and DQ notebooks share the same ID.

scenario_sketches.title: Generic DQ notebook fetch failures clear DqCodeReference and keep DAG creation moving
scenario_sketches.rationale: Generic exceptions are intentionally downgraded to a skip path rather than failing the whole DAG build.

scenario_sketches.title: NotebookException from DQ notebook parsing aborts DAG creation instead of being downgraded
scenario_sketches.rationale: Structural notebook validation errors are explicitly rethrown, so they need a separate failure-path contract from generic fetch errors.

scenario_sketches.title: Keyword-form @fmlv.dq_suite(mlv_name="...") resolves the referenced MLV into a DQ suite mapping
scenario_sketches.rationale: Keyword-form argument extraction is a distinct supported caller shape documented in the new contract comment.

scenario_sketches.title: Positional-form @fmlv.dq_suite("...") resolves the referenced MLV into a DQ suite mapping
scenario_sketches.rationale: Positional-form argument extraction is a second supported caller shape and can regress independently of keyword support.

scenario_sketches.title: DQ suite mappings contain only the DQ cell index and do not prepend common-cell indexes
scenario_sketches.rationale: The new dqSuite storage contract explicitly differs from MLV execution indexing and is independently revertable.

scenario_sketches.title: A notebook cell containing both an MLV create decorator and dq_suite is rejected as a structural conflict
scenario_sketches.rationale: Mixed create-plus-DQ cells now throw a hard structural validation error and deserve their own malformed-input test.

scenario_sketches.title: A notebook cell containing multiple dq_suite decorators is rejected as a structural conflict
scenario_sketches.rationale: Multiple dq_suite decorators in one cell are a separate malformed shape from create-plus-DQ coexistence.

scenario_sketches.title: GetDqSuiteCommandIndexes resolves exact names and exact names that differ only by case
scenario_sketches.rationale: The lookup method lowers the query name before exact dictionary lookup, making case-insensitive exact resolution part of the new contract.

scenario_sketches.title: A fully qualified lookup name can match a shorter stored DQ key by stripping leading query segments
scenario_sketches.rationale: Strategy 2 adds a new fallback path for callers that pass workspace/lakehouse-qualified names against shorter stored keys.

scenario_sketches.title: A shorter lookup name can match a longer stored DQ key, with the longest suffix match winning when multiple keys qualify
scenario_sketches.rationale: Strategy 3 and longest-key selection are separate reverse-direction lookup guarantees from query-side suffix stripping.

scenario_sketches.title: Blank DQ lookup names return null immediately
scenario_sketches.rationale: The new null-or-whitespace guard is defensive code and should be locked down explicitly.

scenario_sketches.title: Unmatched DQ lookup names return null after exhausting all matching strategies
scenario_sketches.rationale: No-match behavior is a distinct observable outcome from blank-input guarding and from successful suffix matches.
